page: Amazon Web Services r6g.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a custom AWS Graviton2 processor. It features 1 dedicated physical core (1 vCPU) based on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture (Neoverse-N1) running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor. There is no local NVMe storage or bundled GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the single-core performance of the Neoverse-N1 processor falls into the average tier, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1491.89. However, due to its single-vCPU configuration, multi-core benchmarks are limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1491.89 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 946.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 19.58 GB/sec for large-block reads and 10.09 GB/sec for large-block writes. Database operations, such as Redis SET, achieve 1.17 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput reaches 1.12 GB/sec.

From a resource-density perspective, the r6g.medium offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel compute capacity. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent single-threaded execution, though the lack of local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps represent notable architectural tradeoffs. The instance is configured for lightweight caching layers, small database nodes, and memory-bound utility services.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.medium is a memory-optimized instance featuring 1 dedicated vCPU powered by an ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides 8.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU resources. In benchmark testing, the single-core CPU performance is moderate, scoring 1491.89 in stress-ng, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reflect the constraints of a single-vCPU footprint. The instance achieves 1.17 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 1.12 GB/sec in static web serving. The r6g.medium represents a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads with low parallel processing demands, such as small database instances, caching layers, and lightweight backend services.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.medium is a memory-optimized instance with 1 ARMv8 Graviton2 vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight caching and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.medium is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 1 vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and Nitro hypervisor technology.
tagline: An entry-level, memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a dedicated core and an eight-to-one memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight caching workloads.
bullet_points: 1 dedicated ARM64 vCPU (AWS Graviton2 Neoverse-N1 at 2.5 GHz)
8.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with no local storage
Delivers 1.17 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Suited for lightweight caching, small databases, and memory-bound tasks
